evals.prompt: scan my codebase for any hand-rolled code that could be replaced by a well-maintained npm package. focus on substantial implementations, not tiny helpers
evals.expected_output: A structured markdown report identifying hand-rolled implementations with library alternatives, confidence levels, migration difficulty ratings, and an Already Installed But Unused section
expectations: The report identifies at least 3 distinct hand-rolled implementations
Each finding includes the file path and line range
Each finding includes a suggested library replacement with npm download stats or GitHub stars
Each finding is rated with a confidence level (High/Medium/Low)
Each finding includes migration difficulty (Easy/Moderate/Hard)
The report has a summary section with total candidates found
package.json was read before scanning code (Phase 1 Inventory)
The report includes an Already Installed But Unused section
The report includes an Internal Duplication section or notes on copy-pasted utilities
The report was saved to a markdown file

evals.prompt: check src/core/ for code that could use a library instead of being hand-rolled
evals.expected_output: A focused report on just the src/core/ directory, identifying hand-rolled utilities that have library alternatives
expectations: The scan was limited to src/core/ (not the entire codebase)
At least 1 finding was identified in src/core/
Findings include specific file paths within src/core/
The report does not include findings from other directories like src/agent/ or src/memory/
package.json was read to check for already-installed alternatives

evals.prompt: audit my codebase for reinvented wheels
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers from this casual phrasing and produces the same structured report as a more explicit request
expectations: The skill was invoked (wheel-detector skill was used)
A structured report was generated with findings
Findings are grouped by confidence level
The report includes a summary section
The report includes an Already Installed But Unused section
